sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Chelsea have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, scoring 8 goals but struggling defensively with no clean sheets. Brighton, on the other hand, have been in decent form with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, scoring an impressive 17 goals, including two 6-0 victories.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Brighton have dominated Chelsea, winning both matches (3-0 and 2-1). Historically, matches between these teams often see both teams scoring and over 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Chelsea rank high in offensive stats, with 10 goals scored (2nd in the league) and leading in big chances created. Brighton, sitting at 15th with 6 goals, have shown attacking flair recently, though their defense remains vulnerable.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Chelsea's home advantage at Stamford Bridge and superior possession stats (62% average) could play a role, but their defensive lapses and Brighton's recent scoring form suggest a competitive match. Yellow cards are likely given both teams' foul counts and the referee's average of 3.4 per game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Chelsea lead Brighton & Hove Albion 1-0 at Stamford Bridge. The home side has dominated much of the game, reflected in their superior ball possession and more frequent entries into the final third and penalty area. Enzo Fernández's headed goal in the 24th minute has given Chelsea the edge. Brighton, despite showing resilience in duels and aerial challenges, have struggled to create meaningful chances in attack, failing to register a shot on target so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea have shown consistent attacking intent throughout the first half, with several spells of dominance, especially in the early stages and around the time of their goal. Their momentum was particularly strong in the opening minutes, where they maintained pressure in Brighton's half. Brighton, on the other hand, had brief moments of attacking momentum, especially towards the middle and latter parts of the half, but their spells were less frequent and lacked the same intensity as Chelsea's. The home side often regained control quickly after Brighton's short bursts forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: Enzo Fernández has been the standout player for Chelsea, not only scoring the decisive goal but also contributing significantly in midfield with his composure and vision. Reece James has also impressed with his defensive contributions and ability to push forward. For Brighton, goalkeeper Bart Verbruggen has kept his team in the game with solid interventions, while Jan Paul van Hecke has been a rock at the back with crucial clearances. However, Brighton's attacking players, like Kaoru Mitoma and Yankuba Minteh, have been largely quiet.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea's approach has been characterized by high possession and a focus on penetrating the final third, which has paid off with their goal. Their ability to win duels and maintain control in midfield has stifled Brighton's creativity. Brighton, meanwhile, have relied on a more defensive setup, looking to counter when possible, but their lack of precision in the final third has been evident. They’ve shown strength in aerial duels, which could be a route to exploit in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: In the second half, Chelsea are likely to continue pushing for another goal to secure the win, leveraging their dominance in possession and attacking intent. Brighton will need to take more risks if they are to get back into the game, potentially opening up spaces for Chelsea to exploit on the counter. The away side might focus on set pieces, given their aerial strength, to try and level the score. Disciplinary issues could also play a role, as both teams have picked up a yellow card each, hinting at a potentially feisty second half.
